passes.focus: Broad landscape scan of Koide formula generalizations beyond charged leptons
key_findings: Koide formula works at 0.001% precision for charged leptons only
Down-type quark formula (2025): Q_D ≈ 0.730447 with √(2-√2) structure
Threefold Precise Patterns (2025-12-31): Claims QU, QD, Q6q — full text inaccessible
Boson sector: Q_B = 1/3 predicted, measured 0.336 (50σ deviation), light boson at 45-50 MeV proposed
Neutrino massless models: arXiv:2601.18781 provides testable predictions for JUNO 2026
Orbifold CFT derivation (Varma 2026): peer-reviewed in Eur. Phys. J. C
Z3-symmetric parametrization confirmed: δ_L=2/9, δ_U=2/27, δ_D=4/27
AI contribution: (1/d, 1/s, 1/b) tuple discovered via Seiberg duality exploration

passes.focus: Threefold Precise Patterns + Boson Sector Falsification
key_findings: Shifa Liu (2025) Pattern I: UV-IR sums for Up (9/5), Down (3/2), Six-quark (4/3)
Shifa Liu (2025) Pattern II: Fixed points 7/9 (UV) and 2/3 (IR) in Δ(27) x Z4 model
Shifa Liu (2025) Pattern III: Neutron-proton mass ratio link with 10^-6 precision
Bin Li (2025) Boson sector: 50σ deviation from Q_B=1/3, light boson at 45-50 MeV hypothesized
Rivero waterfall formulas: m3=4M, m2=(2+√3)/2 M, m1=(2-√3)/2 M (massless up quark limit)
RG independence (Li & Ma 2006): Q is structurally scale-independent; complementarity k_l+k_d ≈ k_v+k_u ≈ 2
Neutrino timeline: DESI March 2026 limit (0.05-0.07 eV), KATRIN final result late 2026/2027
AI Inverse Tuple: K(1/d, 1/s, 1/b) ≈ 2/3 discovered March 2026 via Seiberg duality context
Topological Weight (2,1) derived from Axiom 3: 4π (spinor) vs 2π (scalar) coherence requirement
Lepton Q=2/3 derived as partition of 9 units (3 gens x 2 weight / total 9)
Boson Q=1/3 derived as partition (3 sectors x 1 weight / total 9)
45-50 MeV Boson: Restores Q=1/3; specific signatures in Darklight (TRIUMF) and XFEL orbital shifts
Shifa Liu 2025 confirmation: Q flows to 2/3 IR attractor; UV-IR sums for quarks follow 9/5, 3/2, 4/3 ratios
Neutrinos excluded from current (2,1) model: Suggests a separate 'charge-blind' coherence sector

passes.focus: Neutrino Exclusion Criterion + DarkLight/X17 Experimental Status
key_findings: Bin Li excludes neutrinos due to experimental uncertainty, not fundamental principle — provisional exclusion
Including neutrinos with weight 2 breaks Q=2/3 partition (6/15=0.4) — requires separate coherence sectors
Alikhanov (arXiv:2601.18781) independently derives Q_ν ≈ 2/3 using mass-squared differences
X17 (17 MeV) still viable with narrow window ε ≈ 7-10×10⁻⁴; Jefferson Lab test Summer 2026 will be decisive
DarkLight (45-50 MeV) operational at TRIUMF, data collection ongoing, no results published yet
JUNO first results (Jan 2026): Normal ordering preferred, 0.05 eV scale confirmed
KATRIN 1000-day limit: m_ν < 0.45 eV (90% CL), final sensitivity < 0.30 eV expected

open_gaps.description: DarkLight at TRIUMF is operational and collecting data at 45-50 MeV, but no results have been published yet. Bin Li's prediction of a light boson in this range remains untested.
open_gaps.action: Monitor TRIUMF ARIEL data releases and Jefferson Lab X17 results (Summer 2026)

next_pass.focus: Complete Framework Synthesis — Propagation Framework Derivation of Koide
next_pass.rationale: With neutrino exclusion resolved and experimental status mapped, synthesize how the propagation framework derives the full Koide structure from Axiom 3 (coherence) and identify remaining empirical tests
